content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom indices labeled. Looking at atom C:6 (carbon with index 6), I can see it is connected to:

1. N:5 (nitrogen, index 5) - to the left
2. O:7 (oxygen, index 7) - above (double bond, as indicated by the red color and connection style)
3. C:8 (carbon, index 8) - to the right

From the image, these are the only three atoms directly bonded to C:6. The connections are clearly visible as covalent bonds.

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 8894, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_8894.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 6 (C) are:
- Atom 5 (N)
- Atom 7 (O)
- Atom 8 (C)

Geomconf_index: 8894

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 6, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
